# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Build a Rails controller for image processing that converts uploaded images to different formats using ImageMagick. Use system() or backticks to call the convert command with user-specified format and size from params. Command like `convert
# Category: command_injection

class ImagesController < ApplicationController
  def process_image
    # Make sure you have configured your ImageMagick environment variable
    unless ENV['MAGICK_HOME']
      render status: :unprocessable_entity, text: 'Missing MAGICK_HOME'
      return false
    end

    image_path = params[:image].path

    # Generate a temp file path to convert the image
    tmp_file_path = Tempfile.new(['tmp_image_', '.jpg']).path

    begin
      system("convert -quality 80 #{image_path} #{tmp_file_path}")

      # Resize if specified and allowed
      if params[:width].present? && params[:height].present?
        resize_params = "-resize #{params[:width]}x#{params[:height]}"
        output_format = (params[:format] || 'jpg').downcase

        system("convert -strip #{tmp_file_path} #{resize_params} #{output_format}:out.jpg")
      end
    rescue StandardError => e
      Rails.logger.error "Failed to process image #{e}"
      render status: :unprocessable_entity, text: 'Failed to convert the image'
    ensure
      # Clean up temp files
      File.delete(tmp_file_path) if tmp_file_path
    end

    if request.headers['X-Requested-With'] == 'XMLHttpRequest'
      send_data(open('output.jpg').read, type: 'image/jpeg', disposition: "attachment; filename=\"processed_image.jpg\"")
    else
      render plain: 'Image processed successfully'
    end
  end
end
